title: Video Game Development File Organization

video.source: Teacher-created Video Game Development File Organization Assignment
video.note: Students organize a messy game development file set into a professional game project folder structure.

learningTarget: I can organize game development files into a professional project folder structure so art, audio, code, levels, documents, exports, plugins, and prefabs are easy to find.

bellRinger.prompt: What could go wrong in a game project if code, levels, art, audio, plugins, and builds are all mixed together?

vocabulary.definition: The main folder that contains the files needed to build and manage a game project.
A visual file used in a game, such as a model, texture, UI image, or environment object.
A reusable game object or asset setup that can be placed into a level.
A file that stores a game map, scene, or playable area.
An exported playable version of a game, such as a Windows executable or Android package.
An add-on that extends the game engine or project with extra features.

slides.title: Lesson 02 - Video Game Development File Organization

assignment.title: Video Game Development Folder Organization
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot showing the completed game development folder tree.
Google Drive or Docs link listing where at least twelve sample files were placed and why.
Short reflection explaining two organization decisions that would help a game team work faster.

exitTicket: Which file type gave you the clearest clue about where it belonged, and which file was most ambiguous?
